outcome: Given a bounded world brief, generate, materialize, simulate, evaluate, and iteratively improve a reproducible playable world with coherent terrain, resources, settlements, raidable bases, species, inhabitants, assets, and motion without manual content authoring.

enginePolicy.gatersOwns: Deterministic recipes, semantic generation, stable gameplay identity, diffs, content contracts, evaluators, experiment evidence, and optimizer decisions.
enginePolicy.unrealOwns: Rendering and instancing, mesh/component lifecycle, physics, navigation runtime, animation runtime, profiling APIs, and scalable engine subsystems when their contracts fit.
enginePolicy.adoptionRule: Borrow an Unreal subsystem when it deletes work without replacing deterministic recipe identity; otherwise isolate the smallest adapter and preserve evidence for later replacement.
enginePolicy.currentExceptions: Runtime terrain and content cells are custom and provisional because seed facts are generated on demand. Repeated resource and claim visuals use native ISM rendering and collision with a Gaters full recipe-ID/diff adapter, but player-facing touch feel still needs live visual confirmation. Physical-fit classifies typed terrain contacts and AABB clearance from immutable evidence while keeping attachment contacts explicitly pending; attachment solving, intended embed depth, navigation, and engine collision-shape evidence remain pending. Destructible base pieces intentionally remain unique actors. PCG, World Partition, Mass, Nanite policy, GPU draw-call capture, and animation profiling are not yet integrated.

machines.name: Research Registry
machines.outcome: One authoritative graph owns machine contracts, dependencies, status, verification, champions, and target waves.
machines.verifier: Registry validator checks identity, required fields, dependency targets, waves, and cycles.
machines.challengeSet: Duplicate IDs, missing dependencies, cycles, invalid source/status, and later-wave prerequisites.
machines.failureArtifact: Validation errors naming the machine, field, or dependency edge.
machines.promotionGate: Both agent runtimes read the same valid registry and all overview formats derive from it.
machines.workDeleted: Reconciling separate roadmaps and manually maintained capability diagrams.

machines.name: World Recipe
machines.outcome: A deterministic pure-data world identity exists before Unreal actors and visual assets.
machines.verifier: Unreal automation validates canonical checksum, stable nodes, transforms, and semantic keys.
machines.challengeSet: Repeated seeds, changed seeds, duplicate IDs, missing required arrival site, invalid transforms, mismatched base sites, moved site or route nodes, and changed settlement module, parcel, growth-front, or coordinate-stable path nodes.
machines.failureArtifact: Validation errors plus canonical recipe checksum and node list.
machines.promotionGate: All recipe automation passes and duplicate sweeps produce identical checksums.
machines.workDeleted: Coupling generators, persistence, evaluation, and materialization to spawned actors.

machines.name: Seed-Derived World Intent Recipe
machines.outcome: A seed declares deterministic broad regional terrain, hydrology, vegetation, stone, landmark, and travel-friction intent without requiring detailed cells or universal resource guarantees.

machines.failureArtifact: Seed, generator version, region IDs, coordinate, intended profile, and validation diagnostic.
machines.promotionGate: Held-out seeds reproduce stable valid intent, coordinate ownership remains deterministic, and sparse intent is accepted without a resource-presence guarantee.
machines.workDeleted: Guessing whether unusual terrain or resource absence is intended by the seed or caused by a downstream generator defect.

machines.name: Terrain Family Generator
machines.outcome: Seeds deterministically produce bounded lowland, mountain, canyon, or archipelago landforms with orthogonal dry, lake, river, or ocean hydrology and a playable Arrival neighborhood; optional site candidates are evidence, not a universal guarantee.

machines.failureArtifact: Seed, environment family, sampled terrain profile, failed terrain assertion, and hashed clean/traversal PNG pair.
machines.promotionGate: Every declared-playable Arrival remains escapable, held-out seeds retain deterministic terrain, optional scarcity is accepted, morphology evidence rejects unexplained radial stamps, and the human accepts the fixed-camera seed-131 silhouette.
machines.workDeleted: Hand-authoring the macro terrain for every test world.

machines.name: Intent-Aware Terrain Field
machines.outcome: Streamed terrain samples preserve the seed's existing arrival region while smoothly consuming regional terrain and hydrology tendencies from the authoritative world intent recipe.

machines.failureArtifact: Seed, intent version, region ID, coordinate, influence, global and regional profiles, observed height, and fixed-camera gallery pair.

machines.workDeleted: Building separate terrain generators per biome region or manually deciding where one seed's terrain families transition.

machines.name: Terrain Metrics Evaluator

machines.outcome: Pure height fields expose versioned closed-contour evidence that can distinguish compact radial generator stamps from irregular landforms without deciding whether a requested crater or atoll is valid.

machines.workDeleted: Relying on reachability and random screenshots to notice obvious circular terrain stamps.

machines.name: Intent Terrain Fidelity Evaluator
machines.outcome: Externally supplied terrain observations receive causal evidence that the global core and every regional core match their own seed declaration, without requiring any terrain family or resource to exist universally.

machines.workDeleted: Visually guessing whether a strange region is faithful to its seed declaration or a broken generator output.

machines.name: Regional Water Surface Recipe
machines.outcome: Seed-declared wet regional profiles emit stable bounded lake, river, or ocean render surfaces while dry profiles emit none and existing global water remains unchanged.
machines.verifier: Pure recipe automation covers exact determinism, dry suppression, bounded water extent, containment, and unique stable IDs. Regional lake centers and extents scale to their declared region and the same footprint drives both terrain depression and surface output while global lake geometry remains unchanged. Environment-root validation injects authoritative QueryTerrain evidence into the independent physical-fit evaluator. Held-out runtime-scale seeds 11, 29, 47, 83, and 131 validate and fit their declared surfaces at 65-by-65 drainage evidence resolution; seed 53 retains its generic river cut.
machines.challengeSet: Dry, lakes, river, and ocean regional profiles across terrain families, overlapping wet regions, world boundaries, streamed terrain transitions, and held-out seeds.
machines.failureArtifact: DrainageRealRegionalWaterFitChallenge.log records seed 11 and 29 regional lakes placed where composed influence was zero or partial. DrainageRealRegionalWaterFitGeometryHypothesis.log records the remaining seed 83 sub-grid miss. The passing 65-by-65 challenge is DrainageRealRegionalWaterFit65Challenge.log. Each failure names seed, surface ID, minimum sampled height, datum, and center influence.
machines.promotionGate: An independent water-fit evaluator confirms coverage, terrain intersection, containment, absence in dry profiles, no visible hard-edge leaks, and normal streaming/performance budgets across held-out profiles.
machines.workDeleted: Hand-placing water whenever a seed introduces a wet regional terrain profile.

machines.name: Regional Water Physical-Fit Evaluator
machines.outcome: A pure evaluator rejects missing, leaking, misplaced, or terrain-disconnected regional water surfaces without scoring unfinished water art.
machines.verifier: Two pure evaluator tests accept a correct mixed lakes/ocean recipe and independently reject a missing surface, wrong water datum, dry-region leakage, and an injected composed terrain field elevated above every surface. Environment-root validation passes its authoritative QueryTerrain sampler rather than letting the generator and evaluator share an unblended profile assumption. Runtime-scale seeds 11 and 29 remain permanent composed-terrain counterexamples and now pass without changing datum tolerance.
machines.challengeSet: Dry, lakes, river, and ocean declarations; missing and excess counts; invalid region identity; hydrology mismatch; bounds escape; wrong datum; and no submerged terrain evidence.
machines.failureArtifact: Evaluator version, intent version, seed, region ID, hydrology, expected and actual counts, surface datum and bounds, submerged sample count, and causal diagnostic.
machines.promotionGate: Injected-height corruption tests, runtime-scale composed-terrain counterexamples, and the complete 131-test Gaters suite remain green; material, color, opacity, waves, foam, reflections, and shoreline beauty are explicitly outside this machine.
machines.workDeleted: Opening every wet seed to guess whether water is physically present in the correct region and intersects its terrain.

machines.name: Content Distribution Evaluator
machines.outcome: Externally supplied physical opportunities and semantic placement counts receive independent density, kind-mix, scarcity, and opportunity-correlation evidence without loading assets or asking the generator to score itself.
machines.verifier: Two synthetic tests accept a matching sparse-to-dense distribution and independently reject constant density across varied opportunity plus content inside declared scarcity. A held-out Environment Recipe sweep independently queries physical opportunities and counts generated semantic placements across 66 unobstructed cells from seeds 11, 29, 47, 83, and 131; it records 0.056 mean density error, 0.006 kind-mix error, and 0.781 opportunity-density correlation.
machines.challengeSet: Declared empty, sparse, mixed, dense, vegetation-dominant, stone-dominant, frozen, saturated, dry, coastal, steep, obstructed, and constant-density counterexamples across held-out roots.

ContentDistributionV1Final.log records held-out observation count, density error, kind-mix error, correlation, and causal issue IDs.
machines.promotionGate: Synthetic counterexamples remain rejected and held-out root observations stay within declared error and correlation tolerances without requiring assets, Actors, sites, or universal content presence.
machines.workDeleted: Opening every seed and eyeballing whether sparse and dense content follows its physical environment.

machines.challengeSet: Terrain, villages, bases, species, motion, repeated layouts, catalog swaps, camera distances, and deliberately broken compositions.
machines.failureArtifact: Fixed-camera captures/videos, pairwise labels, evaluator/version, disagreement, and implicated recipe/artifact IDs.
machines.promotionGate: Perceptual improvement holds on hidden galleries without structural, gameplay, diversity, or performance regression.
machines.workDeleted: Opening every world live to decide whether it looks natural, interesting, readable, or repetitive.

machines.name: Face Sculpt Evaluator
machines.outcome: Blind paired evaluation records front-reference likeness separately from three-dimensional plausibility, visual coherence, and individual character without treating one realism or proportion style as globally required.
machines.verifier: Opposite-order evaluator ballots plus a held-out mechanically valid anatomy counterexample; target-matched body-contour and facial-feature receipts independently rank bounded candidates without raster appearance inputs; fresh reopen rejects hidden landmark mappings and additive duplicate shape keys.
machines.challengeSet: Front-only reference ambiguity, label-order bias, projection cheats, generic smoothing, unintended age or proportion drift relative to the declared target, plausible ties, reference/mesh anchor-semantic mismatch, control-region extrema that are not visible landmarks, hidden rear vertices, Blender target-name collisions, annotation provenance, uncertainty margins, and evaluator leakage from an undeclared visual style.
machines.failureArtifact: Packages, renders, ballots, disagreement, evaluator versions, verbatim annotations, geometry receipts, scene locks, invalidated incidents, selection arithmetic, and implicated candidate IDs.
machines.promotionGate: Both blind evaluators reject held-out failures and agree on hidden champion comparisons without claiming unsupported profile likeness.
machines.workDeleted: Unaided subjective claims that a face became closer.

machines.name: Layered World Evaluation Record
machines.outcome: Every world run stores hard failures, metric vectors, diversity descriptors, artifacts, uncertainty, and causal diagnostics without one magic score.
machines.verifier: Curated failures at every layer project into a stable record naming the failed contract and evidence artifact.
machines.challengeSet: Hard failure, partial evaluation, unknown historical field, evaluator disagreement, timeout, and valid diverse candidates.
machines.failureArtifact: The immutable evaluation record itself plus referenced raw evidence.
machines.promotionGate: A failed candidate identifies its layer and next experiment; no missing metric is silently treated as zero.
machines.workDeleted: Opaque pass/fail decisions and rewatching runs to diagnose failures.

machines.name: Face Sculpt Search
machines.outcome: Named Blender facial axes iteratively produce, evaluate, narrow, and preserve champion/challenger face sculpts.
machines.verifier: Replay the bounded run from initial candidate through every proposal, ballot, step update, macro and feature receipt, measured response calibration, invalidated metric or annotation, and retained, nominated, or review-only decision.
machines.challengeSet: Bounds, ties, evaluator disagreement, coupled controls, minimum steps, invalid Blender output, interrupted runs, local optima, controls whose target lies outside the tested span, nonresponsive semantic landmarks, additive duplicate targets, and changes smaller than annotation uncertainty.
machines.failureArtifact: Complete run state, candidate recipes, Blender logs, manifests, renders, ballots, semantic measurements, incidents, and promotion or retention decisions.
machines.promotionGate: The final candidate is reproducible, passes hard checks, beats the initial champion in opposite-order held-out audits, and receives later human approval.
machines.workDeleted: Manual parameter guessing, unrecorded sculpt iteration, and reconstructing generator training evidence after the experiment.

machines.name: Front Relief Fit
machines.outcome: An explicit front silhouette and relative-depth raster mold one closed fixed-topology mesh while preserving connectivity and the unconditioned rear.
machines.verifier: Fresh Blender reopen measures front silhouette IoU, named facial depth ordering, topology identity, rear preservation, finite coordinates, positive face area, and maximum edge stretch.
machines.challengeSet: Balanced, broad, and crooked front masks; over-smoothed landmarks; over-retained sharp depth; additive shape-key composition; silhouette discontinuities; and technically valid but anatomically implausible profiles.
machines.failureArtifact: Input raster, recipe and tool hashes, blend, fixed-camera renders, per-feature depth deltas, topology and stretch metrics, and explicit mechanical and visual decisions.
machines.promotionGate: One fixed recipe passes all mechanical gates on balanced, broad, and crooked inputs; production-head suitability remains explicitly outside this narrow contract.
machines.workDeleted: Manual front silhouette tracing and unaudited guesses that a shallow fitted mask preserved usable topology.

machines.name: Anatomical Front Fit
machines.outcome: A versioned front-reference package produces a fresh editable anatomical Blender head, fixed views, geometry receipt, and separate mechanical and visual decisions with one local command.
machines.verifier: Separate Blender processes build, reopen, and measure the candidate; the orchestrator binds all input and output hashes while visual review remains an independent decision.
machines.challengeSet: Missing or changed source inputs, existing run IDs, invalid candidate recipes, failed Blender stages, feature-ratio misses, mechanically valid but visibly unlike heads, and unsupported profile claims from one front image.
machines.failureArtifact: Immutable contract, run record, stage logs, blend, three renders, feature receipt, mechanical result, visual review, and next isolated experiment.
machines.promotionGate: Held-out front references compile new bounded control recipes that pass mechanical gates and independent visual comparison; replaying an existing recipe alone cannot promote the machine.
machines.workDeleted: Manual reconstruction of how one front-derived face candidate was built, reopened, measured, and rejected.

machines.name: Quality Diversity Archive
machines.outcome: The system retains valid elites across terrain, settlement, base, species, ecology, and visual descriptor niches instead of converging on one winner.
machines.verifier: Synthetic and real candidates test cell assignment, replacement, rebinning, coverage, and descriptor-version changes.
machines.challengeSet: One high scalar score, duplicate candidates, sparse cells, changed descriptors, evaluator upgrades, and invalid candidates.
machines.failureArtifact: Candidate/run ID, descriptor version/vector, cell, incumbent comparison, and archive decision.
machines.promotionGate: Archive coverage and within-cell evidence improve without collapsing distinct valid niches.
machines.workDeleted: Manually curating variety and accepting ten cosmetically different versions of one structure.

machines.name: Optimizer and LLM Researcher
machines.outcome: An optimizer selects parameter candidates while an LLM diagnoses evidence and proposes reproducible next experiments across machines.
machines.verifier: Recorded research sessions replay candidate selection, evidence use, proposals, and promotion decisions on held-out problems.
machines.challengeSet: Sparse evidence, conflicting metrics, evaluator exploits, version changes, repeated failures, local optima, and missing artifacts.
machines.failureArtifact: Researcher/version, input evidence IDs, diagnosis, proposed experiment, result, and rejected alternatives.
machines.promotionGate: The researcher improves held-out archive coverage/quality without bypassing hard constraints or promotion gates.
machines.workDeleted: Manually choosing every seed, parameter adjustment, failure diagnosis, and next experiment.

machines.name: Autonomous Content Researcher
machines.outcome: Content briefs become independently generated, validated, compared, repaired, cataloged, and promoted environment, building, character, rig, motion, species, and presentation artifacts.
machines.verifier: Held-out briefs run end to end and independent contract, mechanical, visual, simulation, and budget evaluators inspect outputs.
machines.challengeSet: New asset family, repair, style variation, different species role, animation vocabulary, catalog replacement, and interrupted generation.
machines.failureArtifact: Brief, all source/compiled artifacts, evaluator records, repair history, catalog decision, and champion comparison.
machines.promotionGate: A new content family enters the catalog without manual modeling, rigging, animation, integration, or silent evaluator regression.
machines.workDeleted: The entire repetitive manual content production and integration pipeline.

machines.name: Explicit Override Roundtrip
machines.outcome: Accepted human or agent moves, replacements, and deletions persist as visible recipe patches across regeneration.
machines.verifier: Move, replace, delete, regenerate, and conflict fixtures check explicit patch replay.
machines.challengeSet: Generator removes a target, asset contract changes, overlapping patches, recipe version change, and catalog withdrawal.
machines.failureArtifact: Base recipe, ordered patches, conflict report, and regenerated recipe checksum.
machines.promotionGate: Every accepted edit reapplies or reports an explicit conflict; no edit silently disappears.
machines.workDeleted: Choosing between procedural regeneration and preserving selected art direction.

machines.name: Autonomous Living World Researcher
machines.outcome: A bounded brief repeatedly yields reproducible, diverse, playable worlds with coherent environments, settlements, bases, species, inhabitants, assets, motion, simulations, diagnostics, and promoted champions while the final visual style remains replaceable.
machines.verifier: Held-out world briefs and seeds run end to end; independent evaluators and blind human audits compare champion generations across versions.
machines.challengeSet: All terrain families, different settlement/base/species compositions, sparse resources, catalog swaps, tool failures, evaluator upgrades, long simulations, and unseen briefs.
machines.failureArtifact: Complete experiment lineage: brief, registry/machine versions, parameters, recipes, artifacts, simulations, captures, evaluation records, archive decisions, and repair proposals.
machines.promotionGate: Held-out archive coverage and within-niche quality improve while hard gameplay, persistence, visual, and performance guarantees remain satisfied.
machines.workDeleted: Manually generating, authoring, integrating, inspecting, diagnosing, and retuning every world and content family.
